title: Minimize the admission of containers wishing to share the host process ID namespace

input_row.title: Minimize the admission of containers wishing to share the host process ID namespace
input_row.description: Do not generally permit containers to be run with the hostPID flag set to true.
input_row.rationale: A container running in the host's PID namespace can inspect processes running outside the container. If the container also has access to ptrace capabilities this can be used to escalate privileges outside of the container. There should be at least one PodSecurityPolicy (PSP) defined which does not permit containers to share the host PID namespace. If you need to run containers which require hostPID, this should be defined in a separate PSP and you should carefully check RBAC controls to ensure that only limited service accounts and users are given permission to access that PSP.
input_row.audit: Get the set of PSPs with the following command: kubectl get psp For each PSP, check whether privileged is enabled: kubectl get psp <name> -o=jsonpath='{.spec.hostPID}' Verify that there is at least one PSP which does not return true.
input_row.remediation: Create a PSP as described in the Kubernetes documentation, ensuring that the .spec.hostPID field is omitted or set to false.
input_row.impact: Pods defined with spec.hostPID: true will not be permitted unless they are run under a specific PSP.
input_row.default_value: By default, PodSecurityPolicies are not defined.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_pod_security_host_pid_namespace_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the command `kubectl get psp` to list all PodSecurityPolicies (PSPs).
- For each PSP, use the command `kubectl get psp <name> -o=jsonpath='{.spec.hostPID}'` to check the value of the `hostPID` field.
- The expected configuration is that at least one PSP should have `hostPID` set to `false` or should not define the `hostPID` field at all.
- Pass criteria: At least one PSP returns `false` or is not defined, indicating that containers are not generally permitted to share the host PID namespace.
- Fail criteria: All PSPs return `true` for `hostPID`, indicating that containers can share the host PID namespace.
